POL-538.title: IMPLEMENT: Wire finalize QC repair-loop gate to Medic escalation
POL-538.body:
## Objective

Fix the gap identified in [POL-537](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-537/analyze-finalize-qc-repair-loop-gate-never-escalates-to): `runFinalize()`'s QC repair-loop gate hard-blocks on an untrusted terminal outcome instead of escalating to the existing, already-working Medic run-health mechanism.

## Context

`src/loop/parent.ts` already calls `appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom()` (from `src/run-health/qc-escalation.ts`) immediately after `runQcRepairLoop()` returns, converting untrusted outcomes (`max-rounds`, `medic-referral`, `all-providers-failed`) into run-health symptoms that a subsequent Medic consult can resolve. `src/finalize/index.ts`'s `runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair()` calls the same `runQcRepairLoop()` and gets the identical result shape, but goes straight to `validateQcRepairLoopGate()` and a hard `process.exit(1)` â€” it never calls the escalation function, so there is no automated recourse when finalize's own QC repair loop can't reach a trusted outcome.

Analysis doc: `smartdocs/raw/analysis/pol-537-finalize-medic-escalation-gap-analysis.md`

## Goal

`runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair()` calls `appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom()` (and, for full parity, `appendQcEscalationSymptoms()` on the initial pre-repair QC result) so untrusted outcomes land in `.polaris/runs/<run-id>/run-health-report.json`, exactly mirroring `src/loop/parent.ts`'s established pattern.

## Scope

* `src/finalize/index.ts`
* `src/finalize/qc-trigger.test.ts`
* `src/finalize/POLARIS.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair()` calls `appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom({runId, clusterId, repairResult, repoRoot})` immediately after `runQcRepairLoop()` returns, before `validateQcRepairLoopGate()`
- [ ] The initial (pre-repair) `runQcAtTrigger()` result is escalated via `appendQcEscalationSymptoms()` for parity with `parent.ts`
- [ ] A test reproduces an `all-providers-failed` repair-loop outcome and confirms a `qc-all-providers-failed` symptom is appended to the run-health report before finalize blocks
- [ ] Existing finalize QC gate tests continue to pass
- [ ] `src/finalize/POLARIS.md`'s "QC repair loop relationship" section documents the escalation call
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize src/run-health

## Ordering

Single child. No dependencies â€” small, precisely-scoped wiring fix.

## Non-goals

* Do not change `src/run-health/qc-escalation.ts` â€” the needed functions already exist and are correct
* Do not add automatic inline Medic dispatch inside finalize (escalating the symptom for a subsequent operator-triggered retry is sufficient, per finalize's manual/operator-triggered model)
* Do not change the QC repair-loop dispatch/timeout behavior from POL-530/531/532/533 or the finding-classification fix from POL-536

POL-537.title: ANALYZE: finalize QC repair-loop gate never escalates to Medic on untrusted outcome

POL-539.title: Call appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom before finalize's repair-loop gate blocks
POL-539.body:
## Objective

Add the missing `appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom()`/`appendQcEscalationSymptoms()` calls in `runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair()` (`src/finalize/index.ts`) so untrusted QC repair-loop outcomes escalate to the run-health report before finalize blocks.

## Context

`src/loop/parent.ts` (lines ~1318-1332) calls both escalation functions immediately after `runQcRepairLoop()` returns:

```ts
appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom({ runId: state.run_id, clusterId: state.cluster_id, repairResult: repairLoopResult, repoRoot });
if (repairLoopResult.final_qc_results.length > 0 && repairLoopResult.rounds_completed > 0) {
  appendQcEscalationSymptoms({ runId: state.run_id, clusterId: state.cluster_id, qcResults: repairLoopResult.final_qc_results, afterRepair: true, repoRoot });
}
```

`src/finalize/index.ts`'s `runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair()` calls `runQcRepairLoop()` and gets the identical `QcRepairLoopResult` shape into `repairLoopResult`, but goes straight to `validateQcRepairLoopGate(nextState, config.qc)` and blocks â€” never calling either function. This means an `all-providers-failed` (or `max-rounds`/`medic-referral`) outcome hit during finalize has no automated Medic recourse, unlike the identical scenario during the normal run loop.

Analysis doc: `smartdocs/raw/analysis/pol-537-finalize-medic-escalation-gap-analysis.md`

## Goal

`runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair()` escalates repair-loop outcomes and post-repair QC results the same way `parent.ts` does, before enforcing the terminal-state gate.

## Scope

* `src/finalize/index.ts`
* `src/finalize/qc-trigger.test.ts`
* `src/finalize/POLARIS.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Import `appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom` and `appendQcEscalationSymptoms` from `../run-health/qc-escalation.js` in `src/finalize/index.ts`
- [ ] `appendRepairLoopOutcomeSymptom({runId: nextState.run_id, clusterId: nextState.cluster_id, repairResult: repairLoopResult, repoRoot})` is called immediately after `runQcRepairLoop()` returns, before `validateQcRepairLoopGate()`
- [ ] `appendQcEscalationSymptoms()` is called for the initial pre-repair `runQcAtTrigger()` result when it doesn't pass, and for `repairLoopResult.final_qc_results` when `rounds_completed > 0`, mirroring `parent.ts`'s condition
- [ ] Test: an `all-providers-failed` repair-loop outcome produces a `qc-all-providers-failed` symptom in `.polaris/runs/<run-id>/run-health-report.json` before finalize exits
- [ ] Test: a `max-rounds` outcome produces a `qc-max-repair-rounds` symptom
- [ ] Existing `validateQcRepairLoopGate` blocking-behavior tests still pass unmodified in outcome
- [ ] `src/finalize/POLARIS.md`'s "QC repair loop relationship" section mentions the escalation call
- [ ] `npm run build` and `npm test` pass

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize src/run-health

## Ordering

First and only child. No dependencies.

## Non-goals

* Do not modify `src/run-health/qc-escalation.ts`
* Do not add automatic inline Medic dispatch inside finalize
* Do not touch the repair-loop dispatch/timeout logic from POL-531/532/533 or the coderabbit classification fix from POL-536
